summary: Authorization, tool execution, and audit runtime for production AI agents.
editor_note: The bet is that agents need permissions before they need more demos. Arcade sits between models and business systems, adds OAuth and IDP-aware authorization, thousands of MCP tools, policy hooks, and an audit trail for every action. Unlike API wrappers that just hand agents a key, it treats each tool call as a governed act on behalf of a user. The hard part is whether security teams standardize on Arcade before the agent frameworks absorb this layer.
why_featured: $60M Series A for agent action security

product_evidence.claim: The official homepage positions Arcade as the runtime between agents and business systems, combining auth, tools, and governance in one layer.
Arcade describes agent authorization where agents act as real users with dynamic permissions, existing IDPs plug in, and credentials stay inside the runtime.
The official site describes a central control plane that records what an agent did, on behalf of which user, in which system, with cloud, on-prem, air-gapped, and hybrid deployment options.
The docs expose a broad tool catalog across productivity apps, communication tools, developer systems, payments, search, sales, databases, and customer support integrations.
The funding announcement says Arcade raised $60M Series A led by SYN Ventures and has shipped more than 8,000 MCP tools for delegated user authorization.

market_context.primary_user: Enterprise AI platform teams, security engineers, and developers putting tool-using agents into production.
market_context.differentiation: Arcade focuses on the action layer after model choice, tying identity-aware authorization, tool execution, policy, and audit logs to each agent tool call.
market_context.why_now: Enterprise agent pilots are moving toward production, where security teams need evidence of who authorized each action and what system was touched.

risks.claim: The open question is whether Arcade can remain the neutral action runtime if MCP clients, cloud vendors, and agent frameworks add overlapping authorization and audit layers.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence, docs, and current Series A coverage.
